title: Search modes — fuzzy matching and AST structural search
purpose: The Search tool currently supports only regex content search and glob file finding. Feature 10 calls for a unified multi-search that combines fuzzy matching (nucleo), AST/structural search (tree-sitter queries), and glob filtering. Vector/semantic search is deferred (NG3 adjacent). This brief adds the missing fuzzy and AST modes.
task: Add nucleo crate for fuzzy file matching. Add tree-sitter S-expression query support for structural code search. Extend SearchTool input schema with mode parameter. Fuzzy mode ranks files by nucleo score. AST mode matches tree-sitter patterns and returns matched nodes with location. Testing: real temp directory tests for both modes.

requirements.title: Fuzzy file matching via nucleo
requirements.spec: SearchTool SHALL support a fuzzy mode that matches file paths against a query string using the nucleo fuzzy matching algorithm.
acceptance: mode='fuzzy' walks directory tree, scores each file path against pattern using nucleo::Matcher
Results sorted by score descending, limited by max_results
Output format: { path: String, score: u32 } per match
Test: create temp dir with 'src/auth/login.rs', 'src/auth/logout.rs', 'src/db/pool.rs', fuzzy search 'login', verify login.rs ranked first

requirements.title: AST structural search via tree-sitter queries
requirements.spec: SearchTool SHALL support an ast mode that matches tree-sitter S-expression query patterns against source files and returns matched nodes with their locations.
acceptance: mode='ast' requires additional input field: ast_query (String) — a tree-sitter S-expression pattern
Parses files using the appropriate tree-sitter grammar (detected from extension, reusing ast.rs grammar loading)
Runs tree-sitter query against the parsed tree
Returns matched nodes with: file path, line number, column, node kind, matched text
Glob filter still applies to restrict which files are searched
Unrecognized file extensions are skipped (not an error)
Test: create temp .rs file with a function, ast_query '(function_item name: (identifier) @name)', verify function name captured

requirements.title: Mode routing in SearchTool execute
requirements.spec: SearchTool::execute SHALL route to the appropriate search implementation based on the mode parameter.
acceptance: mode='content' (default): existing regex search — no change
mode='files': existing glob file finding — no change
mode='fuzzy': new nucleo path matching from R1
mode='ast': new tree-sitter query matching from R2
Unknown mode returns clear error listing valid modes
Test: verify all 4 modes produce results on the same temp directory

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement vector/semantic search — that requires embedding models and is NG3 adjacent
SHALL NOT implement combined mode (using one mode as filter and another as ranker) — that is a future enhancement
SHALL NOT implement AST search across languages in a single query — each file uses its own grammar
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn — all search tests pass including new fuzzy and AST tests
Fuzzy search ranks relevant files higher — verified by test
AST search captures named nodes from tree-sitter queries — verified by test
